entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 26th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/26/sap-developer-news-september-26th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Extensibility Wizard

  • Blog by Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, overview of Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • Blog by Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, on side-by-side extensions with SAP Build process Automation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/creating-side-by-side-process-extensions-from-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13867657
  • Documentation:
    • Setup on BTP: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud
    • Setup on S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b2ee5784ccfc45d5acf42d4c60eb5270.html
  • Antonio Maradiaga’s videos on setting up event mesh for SAP Build Process Automation:
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Delivering events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Process Automation via AEM
  • Devtoberfest Session: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/s-4hana-extensions-start-fast-with-the-new-extensibility-wizard/ev-p/13795541
  • Petoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/petoberfest-2024/m-p/13872559

Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 1: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-1-contest-activities/ba-p/13872705
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview

  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: Coming Soon
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd Community Organized Watch Party

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG
  • SAP Community Watch Zurich: https://community.sap.com/t5/z%C3%BCrich-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-zurich/ec-p/13873047#M1
  • SAP Community Watch Hamburg: https://community.sap.com/t5/hamburg-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-hamburg/ev-p/13875609
  • SAP Community Watch Sao Paulo: https://community.sap.com/t5/s%C3%A3o-paulo-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-s%C3%A3o-paulo/ec-p/13875927#M3
  • SAP Inside Track Bengaluru: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-5th-october-2024/ev-p/13855659

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Extensibility Wizard
2:09 Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap
4:03 Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview
6:56 SAP TechEd Community Watch Party

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 26, 2024.

[Daniel and Pepper] For Petoberfest, I'm here with Pepper, and we're here to talk about the Extensibility Wizard, which was released last week and let users create extensions right from within the UI of their SAP app. For the first release, it will work in S/4HANA Cloud and will let you create custom logic, custom fields known as on stack extensions or custom or extension processes known as side-by-side extensions. [Don't go to sleep.]

One of the big benefits is that it's context aware. So for example, if I'm in the sales order application, it will offer me sales order related event triggers to go with my process. There's a little bit of setup involved. So your S/4 HANA Cloud has to know about your SAP BTP and SAP Build installations. That's fairly simple, just involves destinations, trust and communication arrangements. You also have to set up Event Mesh so that your events from S/4 HANA can arrive to process automation.

Luckily, my esteemed colleague Antonio Larediaga has already created two videos to show you very simply how to set this up. One to get your events from S/4 HANA into Event Mesh and then from Event Mesh to Process Automation. The extensibility wizard will undoubtedly be the talk of TechEd and that's why we've set up a Devtoberfest session where Sesh and Kartik can talk at length about how you set this up and how How do you use it? And you will be able to ask them questions. I will put links to all of the videos, the blogs, and the Devtoberfest session in the description.

[Say goodbye.] Bye-bye. 🐶

[Thomas] It seems like just the other day we were starting our planning for this year's Devtoberfest. And here we are, nearly at the end of the first week of the four-week event. My how's time flown. Our team has been super busy both preparing for SAP TechEd virtual, TechEd on tour, as well as delivering these great Devtoberfest sessions to you. At the end of the first week, we've had a really packed week, great attendance. Up over last year, some more people are finding their way to Devtoberfest, which makes us very happy. We've had some excellent sessions across all the topic days.

Keep in mind that Monday is ABAP and CAP day. Tuesday is our day for tooling, Tooling Tuesday, where we're looking at SAP Build, Build Apps, Build Code, Build Process Automation, and how all these tools are bringing innovations for CAP development, low-code, no-code development, and ABAP development as well. Wednesday is our Integration Day. Thursday is MAD, our Machine Learning, AI, and Data Day. And then finally, Friday is both our Front-end Friday, which looks at SAP UI5 and Fiori and other topics related to front-end tools. But Friday is also our Fun Friday, where we have special activities.

Depending upon when you're watching this news item, There may still be time to enter into Petoberfest. Uh, that is our Fun Friday activity for September 27th. Post a picture of your pet in your work environment and, and you can earn some bonus points as well as have fun checking out everybody else's pet pictures as well.

Next Friday, we're going to have another special event. It's going to be our Techtoberfest day on, uh, October the 4th. And on that day, we're going to be asking you to post pictures of your work environment. Show us your laptop set up, your desk set up, whatever cool kind of thing you have set up in your work environment.

Hey, in week two, we will repeat the same daily track structure that you've that you've experienced in this first week. In fact, all four weeks will follow the same track structure, but our Monday is particularly packed with eight individual sessions across CAP and ABAP. We've got some really great things coming in that day. And it's going to be chock full of content. On Tuesday, I'm particularly interested to see the Build Apps, what's coming soon, where they're going to show both new AI innovations and the introduction of SAP UI5 output for Build Apps. Something that I've personally been looking for, for a while. We've got some cool stuff on the integration day around the event architecture and cloud events. We have content on the mad day around generative AI and using CAP with generative AI as well. So a real generative AI kind of day for that particular Thursday. And then finally, for our front end day, we have building elegant applications with Fiori for and Build Code for Android and an iOS Building applications for iOS as well. So we really have a mobile day focused on both Android and iOS

So we've got quite a selection of content for you all throughout the week and keep in mind once we finish week two we'll go directly into TechEd Virtual brought to you live from Germany And then we'll come back with more Devtoberfest, two more weeks of Devtoberfest after TechEd Virtual. And don't forget to enter into the contest. We have activities every day of the week, either by filling out validation tutorials for what you've just saw in your lecture sessions or completing tutorials that go along with them as well. So we've got lots of fun ways for you to earn points towards the big prize that you could win, win, which is, of course, a trip to one of the TechEd in-person events next year. And keep an eye out for TechEd on Tour, where from the ASUG TechConnect event, we're going to be bringing the winners from last year's Devtoberfest. We'll have some great pictures and videos from that event when it takes place in early November. So we look forward to seeing you in all the Devtoberfest and TechEd activities that are coming your way this month.

[Josh] Hey everybody. Are you as excited as I am that it is almost time for SAP TechEd? That's right. October 8th and 9th, SAP TechEd is happening this year. We're going to be coming to you live from Heidelberg, Germany. But if you can't be with us because it's virtual and you still want to talk with others and meet and watch the entire event, you can organize your own watch party. I know that several watch parties have already been organized for the SAP TechEd keynote, which is an hour and a half. It's going to be Michael Amling, Mohammed Alam, several others are going to be putting lots of great information out in this year's keynote. And right after that, for 30 minutes, me and Nora are going to get together and we're going to be hosting a community welcome.

So lots of watch parties are being organized around the world. The SAP community has several websites that I'll put links to in this video so you can see where others are getting together and maybe you can organize your own watch party as well. You can set it up, you can organize it on the SAP community website, and just let others know that you're going to be getting together to watch the Keynote and the Community Welcome and any other parts of SAP TechEd that you want to watch with others. I'll put a link to the one in Zurich, Hamburg, Sao Paulo, and then I think there's going to be one coming from Bengaluru, there's an SAP Inside Track, an SIT event that is happening on October 5th, and if you register for that, I believe you get an invite to the watch party, which is again happening on October 8th, when SAP's TechEd first day is going to go live at 9 a.m. Central European Time. I don't know where you are, so you have to figure out your own times on yourself. That's it, and I can't wait to see you from TechEd.

entry.title: Export and Import Software Collections in a 3-System Landscape for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Image/data in this blog is from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental.

Overview:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the 3-system landscape consists of three core systems and four tenants, each serving a unique role in the development and deployment process:

Development System:

  • Development tenant (for developer extensibility)
  • Customizing tenant (for business configuration and key user extensibility)

Test System:

  • Test tenant (for validation and testing)

Production System:

  • Production tenant (for live, productive usage)

In a 3-system landscape, you have a development system, test system, and production system. Since development and testing activities are separated into two dedicated systems, it is possible to have advanced development projects in this landscape, enabling developer extensibility options. For more information, see System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Configuration content in the 3-system landscape is provided via SAP Central Business Configuration, which is connected only to the development system. The content updates and upgrades in all systems are handled differently from the software updates and upgrades

Overview of Software and Content Upgrade | SAP Help Portal

Development System

    • Development Tenant: Developer Extensibility
      • What is Developer Extensibility? Developer extensibility allows developers to create and manage development projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Changes made through ADT are recorded in workbench requests.
        • How transport management works in Developer Extensibility:
          • As a developer, you implement custom ABAP code in the development tenant using ABAP Development Tools (ADT). For instance, you may create a custom business object using the ABAP RESTful application programming model.
          • All the changes are recorded on workbench requests, once you have finished the implementation, you can release the developer extensibility transport via ADT (ABAP Development Tools) through the Transport Organizer view in ADT (ABAP Development Tool), For more info See Transport Organizer.  
          • An administrator imports the developer extensibility transport into the test and production tenants using the Import Collection app in SAP Fiori. For more information about your system landscape and change recording, see 3-System Landscape and Transport Management and Change Recording and Transportation. 
    • Customizing Tenant: Business Configuration and Key User Extensibility
      • Business Configuration: Business configuration changes are handled through SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC). This involves scoping, organizational, or country-specific configurations.
        • When you have made your configuration changes, you release the corresponding transport requests by using the app Export Customizing Transports.
        • The SAP content update is merged with your custom configuration content in your SAP Central Business Configuration workspace, and the delta is automatically deployed to the customizing tenant in the development system, and it is – by default – automatically released, this transport request appears as collection in the SAP Fiori app Export Customizing Transports with status “released” under the name “BC Upgrade”
          • How transport management works for Business Configuration.  
            • After completing project scoping in SAP CBC, a transport request is generated when the workspace is deployed to the customizing tenant.
            • The transport request is visible in the Export Customizing Transports app.
            • Take over the ownership of a request with the type of Central Business Configuration by selecting it and selecting Assign To Me. 
            • Note : All Tasks of the request type Central Business Configuration must have the status released. For more info refer Transport Types for Business Configurations and Managing Customizing Requests
      • Key User Extensibility Transport Process: Key user extensibility allows non-technical users to customize applications (e.g., adding fields or modifying UIs) based on business needs.
        • How transport management works for Key User Extensibility:
            • Create a software collection using the Export Software Collection app and assign your extension items to it.
            • Export the collection to transport the extension items.
            • Import the collection into the test tenant via the Import Collection app.For more detailed steps, refer to How to Manage and Export Your Software Collection | SAP Help Portal

Test System

Once all transport requests from the development system (customizing requests, software collections, and workbench requests) are exported needs to import to Test System using the Import Collection App.

  • Customizing Requests: Use the Import Collection app to import released customizing requests from the development system.
  • Developer Extensibility: Release the transport request, workbench request, developer extensibility request from ADT and import it into the test system using the Import Collection app.
  • Key User Extensibility: Open the Import Collection app, select the software collection, and import the extension items into the test tenant.

    Below are the actions that can occur, and which status is the initial status of an action in a collection version, and which status is the result of an action in a collection version.

    • Import -> Imports the collection version into the current system
    • Forward -> Makes the collection version available for import in the production system. This action is only available in the test system
    • Discard -> Discards a collection version so that it can’t be imported into the current system, nor can it be forwarded for import into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. Key User Extensibility and CBC collection versions can’t be discarded.
    • Preimport -> The import validation has errors due to dependencies. You can decide to skip these errors and import your collection version without dependencies. Key User Extensibility and CBC collection versions can’t be preimported.
    • Set as Imported -> This status is set if the error is resolved locally in the import system, For more info, you can refer Importing a Software Collection | SAP Help Portal
     Procedure to Import the collection to test system
    • Open the Import Collection app.
    • Select Collection type from the Collection Type drop-down.
    • Select the collection you want to import, once testing is completed, and everything works as expected, the collections can be forwarded to the production system for final deployment. In a 3-system landscape, you can choose to transport your software collections automatically. 
      • Transport Automation: - In the Import Settings, choose the software collections you want to maintain the automation settings for. You can also choose to maintain the automation settings for SAP Scheduled Collections: These are collections scheduled by SAP, such as product-relevant updates that are in specific transports to reach the production system. Choose on which days you want the transport automation to be activated. Then, choose the earliest start time for your import. You can determine the number of hours within which the automated import should start. The start of the automated import depends on the system utilization. At the end of the automated import time range, the import process of collections won't end immediately. It'll still finish running the imports but not start new ones from the transport queue. For more info, you can refer the below help document Transport Automation | SAP Help Portal

Production System

After successful testing, the transport requests are forwarded to the production system. The process is as follows:

  • Customizing Requests, Software Collection, and Workbench Requests, CBC requests are forwarded from the test tenant using the Import Collection app.
  • Once forwarded, you can import them into the production tenant through the Import Collection app.

For more details, refer Importing a Software Collection to the P-System.

Conclusion
The transport process in a 3-system landscape for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud ensures a smooth transition of software collections, business configurations, and extensibility changes from the development environment through to the production environment. Understanding the flow of workbench requests, customizing requests, and software collections helps maintain system integrity and ensures that all changes are appropriately tested before going live.

For 3-System Landscape related to Extensibility and Transport Management, you can refer to the following blogs and KBAs:-

  • Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public E... - SAP Community
  • How to Manage and Move Transport Request in S/4HAN... - SAP Community
  • Export and Import Software collections in S4 Hana ... - SAP Community
  • Extensibility in the Development System of a 3-Sys... - SAP Community
  • Managing Software Collection transports during the... - SAP Community
  • 3126032 - Export/import Software Collections in S/4HANA Cloud - Frequently Asked Questions - SAP for Me
  • 3482742 - Transport Management in S/4HANA Cloud - Central KBA - SAP for Me
  • Tools overview: Tools and Apps | SAP Help Portal
  •  Transport Dependencies | SAP Help Portal
  • Transport Management Summary | SAP Help Portal
  • 3116689 - Export Software Collection - Best Practice - SAP for Me
  • 2638029 - ATO: How to resolve Dependencies of ATO items - SAP for Me
  • 3038415 - Key user extensibility in S/4HANA Cloud is temporarily disabled - SAP for Me
  • 3040585 - In-APP Extensibility and ATO transports are temporarily disabled - SAP for Me
  • 2283716 - Key User Application is not configured - SAP for Me
  • Simple Transport Mode in 3-System Landscape Transp... - SAP Community
  • System Landscape and Transport Management | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Asset Revaluation & Acquisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is intended to complement my previous blogs on automation and augment Finance Asset Accounting scenario with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition as described with available scope items for Asset Revaluation (J62) and SAP Help documentation

A Professional Services company wanted to streamline its operations, particularly in managing the intricate logistics of end-to-end fixed asset acquisition and posting asset revaluation processes. 

Company discovers asset revaluation with seamless automation. By effortlessly collecting data from Asset Accountants through various document formats, including PDFs, we streamline the entire process.

In comprehensive approach, company identify assets acquired both in the current fiscal year and previous fiscal years. Each revaluation includes meticulous details such as Ledger information, Fixed Asset Revaluation Acquisition Amounts, Current Year Amounts, and the increments by which the original acquisition costs (APC) are adjusted.  In addition, specific details around corresponding cumulative value for ordinary revaluation are included.

SAP Build Process Automation's low-code solutions, designed to empower users to create and manage processes and workflows effortlessly. With the no-code process builder, enhancing enterprise applications and business sites is as simple as drag-and-drop.

 

Automation for asset accounting is driven by SAP Build Process Automation Actions (API), and rapidly developed UI-based automations for both SAP and third-party applications using the built-in automation recorder.

Decision management capabilities transform corporate policies and business rules into repeatable processes. The intuitive business rules editor lets users create decision tables, similar to spreadsheets, or text-based rules.

To streamline access and enhance operations, SAP Build Process Automation offer a unified launchpad and task center, providing a seamless user experience. This allows business users to easily access and manage notifications and tasks from various solutions, whether via web or mobile.

In a custom scenario evaluated by the Professional Services company, SAP Build Process Automation demonstrates how it can simplify process and task automation for business users without any coding required. The platform's built-in AI capabilities facilitate intelligent document processing, effortlessly extracting data from both structured and unstructured documents, and seamlessly transferring it to your enterprise systems.

In specific customer business use case, SAP Build Process Automation enhance accuracy by using document templates instead of relying solely on pre-trained models. This is achieved by uploading the business document to the service and creating a document template artifact, which can extract data from various file types (e.g., PDFs) as required for the specific business case.

Company evaluates the SAP Build Process Automation to elevate the efficiency and precision of their Asset management business operations, providing a robust solution for intelligent document processing and seamless integration with enterprise systems.

Enterprise Automation with SAP serves as the foundational layer for integrating Generative AI into your business processes. By embedding AI models into orchestrated workflows and task automations, you can significantly enhance the level of automation and efficiency in your operations. You can learn more with roadmap items

In future enhancements, customer plan to explore the benefits of AI-powered artifact creation and recommendations, areas not covered in the current scope.

  • Generative AI will streamline the creation of process and rules artifacts, leading to more efficient process analysis, automation, and integration capabilities.
  • AI-powered tools will generate integration flows that support seamless scaling and address deviations through advanced anomaly detection.

You can stay ahead of the curve by leveraging the latest AI advancements to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of your business operations.

Furthermore, Event-Driven Architectures with SAP Build Process Automation empower business users to fully automate processes in response to business events. (see Business Accelerator Hub for more details).  You can utilize existing business event objects or design custom ones to monitor and subscribe to specific occurrences within your system of records, proactively triggering automations.

SAP Build Process Automation seamlessly integrates Document Information Extraction capabilities, effortlessly extracting relevant data from both structured and unstructured documents, such as scans.

Key data points, including asset acquisition date, useful life, business transaction type, and debit/credit indicators, are captured to facilitate year-end closing and the display of transactions in the asset history sheet.  It adeptly recognizes whether a revaluation is an increase or a reduction, and accurately extracts Revaluation Amounts for both APC (Acquisition Costs) and Ordinary Depreciation.

Leveraging AI-based document processing, SAP Build Process Automation enhances document extraction with features like automatic template detection and prebuilt activities for efficient data extraction. The system also can support data enrichment, like linking asset and company information from templates to match codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, and can automatically align text.

This AI-driven approach to document information extraction and recommendations underscores customer’s commitment to improving their end-user experience and maintaining a competitive edge in the market.

Consider a typical business scenario where automating asset revaluation and verifying asset posted transactions in specific ledger journal entries is essential for an Asset Accountant.

Using SAP Build Process Automation's tasks, it becomes reality to leverage API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (API_FIXEDASSETREVALUATION_G4BA) to streamline the revaluation process for multiple assets in a single request. This not only enhances data accuracy but also reduces costs compared to manual revaluation entries.

API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation”

The automation action task accesses the API, incorporating nearly all fields from the Fiori app “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (F7775), allowing Asset Accountants to re-valuate assets for selected ledgers and depreciation areas. Revaluations can be applied to acquisition and production costs (APCs) and ordinary depreciation amounts.

To re-valuate an asset, specify the revaluation amount by which the original APCs are to be increased and the corresponding cumulative value for ordinary depreciation. The system then posts the increased APC and ordinary depreciation for the selected ledgers and depreciation areas.

Acquisition and Production Costs encompass any asset acquired or purchased externally, including the invoice price and associated expenses such as customs, octroi, and freight. By factoring in these additional costs, you can determine the total acquisition cost essential for the capitalization of the asset.

Learn more about this efficient automation process by exploring the SAP Help API Documentation.

Next, the Asset Accountant wants to validate the revaluation postings with a list of journal entries and understand the depreciation applied to each asset using an explanation of the depreciation key configuration.

SAP Build Process Automation enables the automation of complex scenarios with ease using the recorder. By automatically capturing the "Manage Fixed Asset" application, it allows for the simultaneous design of automations, leveraging enhanced recording features. These features include recording applications combining screens on SAP GUI for Windows and UI automation technologies, enabling a seamless session that includes both Windows and Web (including UI5) interfaces.

Using a UI-based automation bot scheduled at the desired business frequency, the bot navigates to the “Manage Fixed Assets (New)” Fiori App for specific assets with revaluated postings. The bot executes UI-based navigation with a scheduled job to generate and export the list of journal entries for revaluation postings. This list includes details such as Asset Transaction Type, Ledger, Line Item, Posting Date, Valuation Date, and references, which are then emailed to business users including the Asset Accountant.

Additionally, the bot emails the Asset Accountant and defined business users an AI-assisted explanation of the Depreciation Key Configuration. This provides a better understanding of how depreciation keys operate and the rationale behind the system’s complex depreciation calculations for the specific asset.

An AI-powered, user-friendly explanation of depreciation keys facilitates quicker asset onboarding, enhances business user productivity in tracking asset value throughout its lifecycle and revaluation postings, and streamlines periodic activities. This efficiency reduces the time required to understand depreciation and asset values, driving overall operational effectiveness.

AI-assisted Explanation of Depreciation Key Configuration  (J62 - Beta) 

This AI powered feature is available only  BETA Customers & Partners and you can  find more information, on how  Get Involved Early and register @ Influence portal and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

In this business use case, the company provides assets, such as laptops and ergonomic office furniture, to consultants and project resources. Asset Accountants face the ongoing challenge of entering newly acquired assets and their inventory numbers and IMEIs into the system before distribution and activation for depreciation.

To address this, the company evaluates and customizes the SAP Build Process Automation Bot template to suit the specific needs of Asset Accountants for mass asset acquisition and creation.

The Asset Accountant uploads a pre-filled template to trigger an approval workflow, sending a notification to the Asset Manager for review. Upon the Asset Manager's approval, the automation posts the acquisition of assets in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. The Asset Accountant then receives a detailed report.

Mass Fixed Asset Acquisition SAP Build Process Automation Template

Additionally, SAP Build Process Automation template content streamlines the posting of asset acquisitions by fetching existing unposted fixed assets from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system into a template file, which users update with the necessary asset information. The updated file is then used to extract asset data and utilize the Fixed Asset Acquisition APIs to post the assets. This automation not only saves time and reduces manual errors but also improves overall efficiency. For more details, refer to the documentation on the  SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

As the company continues to acquire new assets, it must create and maintain main and sub-asset numbers on an ongoing basis. To support this, SAP Build Process Automation includes API-driven action tasks to facilitate the creation of main asset number (Learn more about Fixed Asset - Create Main Asset (Asynchronous) with API Documentation, create an sub asset Fixed Asset - Create Sub Asset (Asynchronous) (Learn more with API Documentation)

In specific scenarios, Asset Managers need to update existing assets to reflect changes in master records and valuation information.

SAP Build Process Automation’s API-based Action Task automates these updates, including modifications to the company code, main asset number, asset sub-number, origin information, account assignment, ledger details, valuation data, and time-based valuation information. This streamlined process ensures accuracy and efficiency. For more details, refer to the Fixed Asset - Change (Asynchronous). API documentation .

For more information about the extensibility of the Manage Fixed Assets app, see App Extensibility: Manage Fixed Assets (Documentation)

Visit the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to learn more about 3900+ prebuilt integration scenarios, 1300+ business events, over 250+ connectors to SAP and non-SAP applications, 1200+ automation content, and 35+ integrations all in one place, the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

Follow us via @ash_G and #S4HANA, or myself via@har1234 (Hardeep Tulsi)

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

 Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-exclusive-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2408/ba-p/13757250
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Learn more about GROW here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow.html

  • Check out the SAP Activate Roadmap for early adopters here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapOvervie...
  • Review the PPL content here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContent...

entry.title: The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?
entry.content:
When creating clean core extensions for SAP S/4HANA, you are confronted with a vast number of powerful tools. The tools can be divided into four quarters by the dimensions on-stack versus side-by-side and low code versus pro-code, which is shown in the following figure:

The set of available tools may be overwhelming at first sight, so how to find the right one? And – if you found the right tool, how do you find the right API or extension point to extend the application you are interested in?

Of course, there are documents that provide guidance on when to use what (see for example in the document Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions , Chapter 3, “When To Use What”), but it is definitely better to get the guidance right in the system when creation the extension. SAP is investing a lot in implementing guidance in the system. The investment goes along two paths:

  • Investment in AI support (Joule)
  • Investment in wizards

Many new wizards are available in the ABAP platform, for example for creating a new RAP BO or for creating custom fields. For a full picture, I refer to other documents and presentations given during TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

In this blog post I want to introduce the Extensibility Wizard. This wizard guides you from the application through the creation of the respective extension. Here is an example (instead of reading the following lines, you can also watch my demo in my TechEd or Devtoberfest session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility 😞

  1. Assume that you are in your SAP S/4HANA application. For my example I choose the application Manage Journal Entries.
  2. Open the Fiori Launchpad context menu (click your avatar). If the Extensibility Wizard is configures (see link to the documentation with the setup guide at the end of this post), you can see the menu entry Create Extension, and you can click it (Figure 2).

                  
                  Figure 2

  1. The Extensibility Wizard opens and shows the different extensions tasks, that you can create for this application. As of 2408, the following extensions tasks are included:
    1. Custom Fields
    2. Custom Logic
    3. Custom Process
    4. The figure below shows – as a labs preview – also the extensibility task Custom Application, which is not yet available in your 2408 release.

               
               Figure 3

  1. Now let us select the extensibility task Custom Process. The next steps of the wizard now guide us through the creation of a new process in SAP Build Process Integration. As the wizard was started from the Manage Journal Entries application, it knows which triggers are relevant for this application, and offers for example the event JournalEntry Created as the trigger for a new process. As a final step, the wizard creates the new process in SAP Build and let you navigate to SAP Build Lobby and directly into the process. (Please see the complete flow in my session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility )

I hope that this example gives you an idea on how the Extensibility Wizard works.

As mentioned, in 2408, the Extensibility Wizard includes – apart from the Custom Process – two more tasks:

  • The Custom Field task opens the Custom Fields Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create a custom field. Also here, the business context is prefilled according to the application from where you start the wizard.
  • The Custom Logic task opens the Custom Logic Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create custom logic. Also here, the business context is prefilled.

If you are used to open the Custom Fields and the Custom Logic apps via the UI Adaption Mode (menu entry Adapt UI, as you can see in Figure 2), you can of course still do this.

In future additional extensibility items and features will be added, for example:

  • For the extensibility item Custom Process, additional trigger options will be provided.
  • A new extensibility item Custom Application will be added, which guides you through the creation of a new application with SAP Build Code.

Curious to see the wizard in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant? Configure it today, see the following blog, video and documentation:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud
  • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d1Uy5PEwbtc&t=1954s

entry.title: Release Assessment and Scope Dependency for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition : Partner TDD Systems
entry.content:
Announcement

The release assessment and scope dependency tool has been providing our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers with disruption-free upgrades ensuring zero Day 1 impact since 2019. We have been tirelessly working to improve the release assessment and scope dependency tool to keep up with the latest customer demands

We have also received multiple requests from our Partner community to enable release assessment and scope dependency for Partner TDD systems. We are pleased to announce that with the latest version of the tool, we have now included Partner TDD systems as part of release assessment and scope dependency.

This means Partners can now log in with their Partner S-User IDs to view content related to their Partner TDD Customizing (100) client within the tool. Partners will be able to view content in release assessment and scope dependency in the same way customer data is represented.

You can find the list of all contents related to release assessment and scope dependency here. Additionally, you can check out what’s new in release assessment and scope dependency for 2024 here.

entry.title: SAP BTP BRH (Batch Release Hub for Life Sciences) Application Extension
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Batch Release Hub for Life Sciences is your central point of access to the information necessary for

making fast and accurate release decisions in the pharmaceutical industry.

As a person involved in the release decision process, SAP Batch Release Hub for Life Sciences provides you with the information you need to prepare, review, and make compliant release decisions. By centralizing the relevant data from diverse source systems in one place, the solution helps you manage the complexity of the release decision process and detect any deviations early in the process.

 

Integration Options

Depending on business needs, SAP Batch Release Hub for Life Sciences can be integrated with a variety

of source systems such as follows:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals
  • Laboratory information management system (LIMS)
  • Regulatory information management system (RIMS)
  • Quality management system (QMS)
  • Manufacturing execution system (MES)
  • SAP Global Batch Traceability (SAP GBT)
  • SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management

 

In our case we are integrating SAP BRH with S/4HANA.

 

Architecture

  

Requirement.

SAP BRH APPLICATION UI EXTENSTION.

 

  1. Introduce a few new fields with possible values Stop Shipment, SUQ, Obsolete which is mapped from the S4 app.

 

Field ID

Field Description

Type

Length

ZZ_Reason_Rest

Reason for Restriction

String

200

ZZ_Batch_Status

Batch status

String

1

 

  1. Below are the CDS data models need to extend to achieve the same
  • BatchesCore
  • BatchesService
  1. Below are the fields need to be added while extending entity for BatchesCore and BatchesService
  • ZZ_Reason_Rest - Reason for Restriction
  • ZZ_Batch_Status - Batch status
  1. Extend the BatchesStage and BatchesActive UI to for extended fields.

To achieve the above requirement we are going to extend UI and DB table SAP BTP.

  • Frontend extension (UI) – to extend the view
  • Extending Native SAP S/4 HANA Integration

 

Development

Frontend Development on BTP.

 

  1. To Extend the field we should have BAS (Business Application Studio) service activated BTP Sub Account.
  2. Once we have service activated, we should login into the cloud foundry and start the development.

              

 

 

  1. Open The Terminal and install the appropriate version of @Priya0/cds-dk as follows

Version of SAP Batch Release Hub for Life Sciences

SDK Version

Command

2406

7.7.2

npm install -g @Priya0/cds-dk@7.7.2

 

 

 

  1. Create a new Directory / Project into the BAS through UI / below command also  can be used to create directory.

Command to create Directory - mkdir brh_ext_company_short_name_01

Go inside the rectory and execute command - cd brh_ext_company_short_name_01

 

  1. After creating Directory please create a file ( package.Json)  and mention the project which you want to extend.

Below is the command to achieve the same.

touch package.json and after that put below JSON into the file.

{ "name": "brh-ext-1",

  "extends": "_base" }

 

Note – Above Step is very important step to pull the project otherwise it will give error.

  1. To extend any project we should have Extension.cds file inside the app folder. Lets create that too with the command, you can do it through the UI also.

“Mkdir app” and once folder created go inside the folder and execute “touch Extension.cds” command.

 

  1. Similarly create a i18n folder and under this create file called i18n.properties

Now the important step is to pull the project which you want to extend. Use the below command for the same.
Syntax - cds pull -s <subdomain> --from <brh-mtx-url>

 

 

Command - cds pull -s ***-mt-dev-eu --from https://iron-preprod-preprod-iron-     mtx.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com

Password -  when will you start pulling the project it will ask for the password.

You can get a valid passcode at:

Syntax - https://<subdomain>.authentication.<landscape>.hana.ondemand.com/passcode

Command - https://***-mt-dev-eu.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/passcode

  1. Once you are done with extension in Extension.CDS file and i18n file lets buid the project and push it to repository / server. With the following command.

Command – CDS Build

 

Once your build is finished you will get the following message like build completed in 2369 ms

at the end.

   

 

 

  1. After Build lets push the project to the Cloud / Server.

Syntax - cds push -s <subdomain> --to <brh-mtx-url>

Command - cds push -s ***-mt-dev-eu --to https://iron-preprod-preprod-iron-mtx.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com

Password -  will generate the password in same manner as pulling the file / project.

Syntax - https://<subdomain>.authentication.<landscape>.hana.ondemand.com/passcode

Command - https://***-mt-dev-eu.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/passcode

 

 You will get Activation succeeded message in couple of second after entering the command. Now your changes are live please check in to the application.

Integration (Extending Native SAP S/4HANA Integration) Development

Extensibility provides customers with the flexibility to adapt the SAP Life Sciences defined model to

accommodate custom attributes that are specific to the business on master and transaction data objects.

The goal of extensibility is to enable customers to extend APIs with custom data. A simple case would be adding single fields, a more complex case would be to add array fields or add child entities.

For the extensions to work seamlessly, customers should make the changes in the Integration Hub, SAP S/4HANA, and Batch Release Hub tenant keeping the field names the same across all the applications.

 

Extensions for SAP BTP

This section contains information about creating customer extensions for Integration Hub for Life Sciences in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

Customers can access all base entity models in the application and can add non-key and non-mandatory fields to the entity by extending the model. These additions should be on the root level of the entity. The field names are case-sensitive. The following default prefixes are allowed for custom attributes:

  • Z_
  • ZZ_
  • Y_
  • YY_

Note - Any other prefix will result in an activation error.

 

Prerequisites

  • You have read the Cloud Application Programming Model documentation (Extending SaaS Applications ).
  • You have been assigned the required role. You must be assigned with the CDS Extension Development access role collection for Integration Hub for Life Sciences, because it contains the cds.ExtensionDeveloper scope, which is required by the cds.xt.Extensibility service. Otherwise, you will get a "403 forbidden" error.
  • You have set up your development environment as follows:
  • We recommend using Nodejs 18 or higher.
  • You must use @Priya0/cds-dk version 7.0.0 or higher.
  • Install and update @Priya0/cds-dk globally by running npm i -g @Priya0/cds-dk@^7.

 

Development

  1. Log on to the BAP BTP BAS (Business Application Studio) and in Cloud foundry.
  2. Create another project in same way we did for frontend model extension:

               Project Name (Folder)

                             DB(Folder)

                             Node_modules(Folder)

                             Package.json(File)

          

 

                  

  1. Once structure is ready. Let’s add the code below to the package.json file. And add extension.cds file in DB folder.

{

    "name": "ihls-extension-project",

    "version": "1.0.0",

    "description": "IHLS extension project",

    "cds": {

        "extends": "_base"

    },

    "dependencies": {

        "@sap/cds-dk": "^7.7.2"

    }

}

 

 

  1. Once your structure is ready let’s pull the project in the same manner we did above. Run the below command line.

Syntax - npx cds pull -s <tenant-subdomain> --from <ihlssubscribed-core-app-url>

Command - npx cds pull -s ***-mt-dev-eu --to https://***-mt-dev-eu-validation-lsifs -core.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com

 

  1. After pulling project lets start development

Since we are extending Batch entity lets find entity in S4 side , for this go to the index.csn file in node_modules folder and search for the Batch, and copy the sap.ihls.batch.Batch.

Please see the screenshot below for the same.

 

 

  1. Now lets modify the extension.cds file in DB folder, and the entity and fields like below.

using {

    sap.ihls.batch.Batch,

} from '_base';

extend entity Batch with {

 

    ZZ_ReasonRest  : String;

    ZZ_BatchStatus : String;

}

 

Once this development is done, lets push the code with below command.

 

Syntax - npx cds push -s <tenant-subdomain> --from <ihlssubscribed-core-app-url>

Command - npx cds push -s ***-mt-dev-eu --to https://***-mt-dev-eu-validation-lsifs -core.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com

 

 

 

 

 

After pushing the code to the server now you should see S4 data in the added field.

 

Note – Don’t forget to enhance BADI in the backend(S4) side for added fields. 😊

entry.title: Integrate SAP Build Process Automation with Custom Business Object from S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Overview

This post is an update on the blog to setup an extension scenario with on-stack and Side-by-Side Extensions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

The use case shown here is creating a Bonus Plan application in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud as a Custom Business Object. This application will initiate the SAP Build Process Automation workflow, allowing the manager to Approve/Reject newly produced Bonus Plans.

Bonus Plan application which is a Custom Business Object and its ABAP Business Logic is developed as an In-App Extension running on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, whereas the workflow is developed using SAP Build Process Automation which is the unified offering in SAP BTP that combines the capabilities of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA and its starting point can be FORM or API. You can read more on SAP Build Process Automation (here).

Note: The Bonus Plan app shown in the exercises is for educational purpose only and not meant for productive usage. It is not ensured that the data used in this extension scenario is managed in accordance with applicable legal requirements for all countries and industries and in accordance with the business needs, such as data life cycle requirements.

Prerequisite:
1.Subscription of SAP Build process automation in BTP Trial Account or BTP Enterprise Account
2.Your user needs a business role with business catalog " Extensibility – Custom Business Objects (ID: SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_CBO)” in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Let’s start on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud side with the definition of the Custom Business Object and the communication settings.

Custom Business Object

Define the data structure and create the database persistence of a custom business object.

  1. Enter your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system’s Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Start typing Custom Business Objects in the Launchpad search and open the App from the results.
  3. Press New to start the creation of a custom business object.
  4. Enter the Name BonusPlan. The other editable fields will be filled up automatically with suggested values but can be changed. From Features section Determination and Validation, User Interface and Back End Service to be selected. With the User Interface option, it generates an SAP Fiori based UI for your Custom Business Object and with the Back End Service option, it generates an OData API that you can expose and access outside with the help of communication arrangement. 

5. Switch to the Fields tab. On the BONUSPLAN node we need the following fields:

Here Approver field has association to Business Object “WORKFORCEPERSON_VH”.

6. The Release Status is linked to the following Custom Code List:

  • To create "YY1_RELSTAT00" custom code list launch "Custom Reusable Elements" app from the Fiori Launchpad.
  • Under Custom Code Lists, choose Add (+) and enter the following details:
  • Name: RELSTAT00, Code List ID: YY1_RELSTAT00
  • Under CODE VALUES, create the Items as mentioned in the picture.

With these settings you have defined the data structure of the Custom Business Object out of which the corresponding database tables and CDS Views will be created during Publish. Publish the business object to trigger the generation of UI (Master and Detail) and OData Service. 

  • Select the just added Catalog and Publish it.

  • open the ABAP editor for the Request Approval action by clicking the Request Approval link and here we can add logic to trigger workflow in SAP Build Process Automation

Initially the action is empty, so you add the following coding.

Note: 3 where we have mentioned how to get the workflow definitionId and context payload structure for triggering the Business process workflow in the BTP.

*Here we are using CL_SWF_CPWF_API_FACTORY_A4C API class to start workflows in SAP Build Process Automation. For more info, please refer to this link.

 

 

* Action RequestApproval for Node ID BONUSPLAN
** Importing Parameter : association (Navigation to Parent/Child/Associated Node Instances)
*  write (API for creating and updating Custom Business Object Node Instances)
* Changing Parameter  : BONUSPLAN (Current Node Data)
* Exporting Parameter : message (Message with Severity S(uccess), W(arning), E(rror))  

* Get the API of workflow.
  DATA(cpwf_api) = cl_swf_cpwf_api_factory_a4c=>get_api_instance( ).
  DATA Wf_handle     TYPE sysuuid_c32.

CONSTANTS: wf_definitionId   TYPE if_swf_cpwf_api=>cpwf_def_id_long  VALUE '****************************.custombonusplan.bonus_change_wf',   
             wf_retention_days TYPE if_swf_cpwf_api=>retention_time VALUE '30',
             consumer          TYPE string VALUE 'DEFAULT'.

**** Define the structure of the payload
  TYPES: BEGIN OF context, 
           approveremail     		TYPE string, 
           id                		TYPE string,
           Bonus             		TYPE string,  
           EmployeeID        		TYPE string, 
           EmployeeName  		TYPE string, 
           ReleaseStatus     		TYPE string, 
           releasestatustext 		TYPE string, 
           DecisionNote      		TYPE string,
           TargetRevenue    		TYPE string,   
           sapuuid           		TYPE string,
         END OF context.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_context,
           context TYPE context,
         END OF ty_context.

 TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_payload,
           start_event TYPE ty_context,
 END OF ty_payload  .

* Get the Email Address for the Approver
  SELECT SINGLE from I_BUSINESSUSERVH FIELDS DEFAULTEMAILADDRESS
    WHERE BPIDENTIFICATIONNUMBER = @BONUSPLAN-approver
    INTO (approvermailid).

* Get the SAP UUID for the BONUSPLAN instance
SELECT SINGLE from YY1_BONUSPLAN FIELDS sap_uuid
    WHERE id = @BONUSPLAN-id
    INTO (uuid).

NEW cl_system_uuid( )->convert_uuid_x16_static(
       EXPORTING
          uuid     = uuid
       IMPORTING
          uuid_c36 = DATA(lv_c36 ) ).
  TRY.
* Get the API of workflow
      DATA(lo_cpwf_api) = cl_swf_cpwf_api_factory_a4c=>get_api_instance( ).
*
**convert workflow data to be passed in json format
**Make sure the approvermailid should be Assigned to Process Automation Roles in the BTP
      DATA(context)   = VALUE type_context(
            context-approveremail    = approvermailid	
             context-id              	= bonusplan-id
            context-bonus           	= |{ bonusplan-targetbonus_v }|
            context-employeeid      	= |{ bonusplan-employeeid }|
            context-employeename   = |{ bonusplan-employeename }|
            context-releasestatus  	 = |{ bonusplan-releasestatus }|
            context-releasestatustext = 'Approval Required'
            context-decisionnote    	 = ''
            context-targetrevenue      = |{ bonusplan-targetrevenue_v }|
            context-sapuuid               =  |guid'{ lv_c36 }'|
             ).

      DATA(context_json) = lo_cpwf_api->get_start_context_from_data(
        iv_capital_letter  = abap_false
        it_name_mapping    = VALUE #( ( abap = 'CONTEXT' json = 'bonusContext' ) )
        iv_data = context
      ).

*trigger workflow
      DATA(cpwf_handle_id) = lo_cpwf_api->start_workflow_ext(
              iv_cp_workflow_def_id = wf_definitionId
              iv_context            = context_json
              iv_retention_time     = wf_retention_days
              iv_do_commit          = abap_true
            ).
      wf_handle = cpwf_handle_id.  
    CATCH cx_swf_cpwf_api INTO DATA(lx_exc).
      message = VALUE #(
    severity = co_severity-success
    text     = 'Action ApproveBonus failed'
  ).

ENDTRY.
message = VALUE #(
    severity = co_severity-success
    text     = 'Action ApproveBonus executed'
).

 

 

 

Creation of a Communication Arrangement for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows

To integrate custom business object from SAP S/4HANA system with External Systems or Services like SAP Build Process Automation you need to create a Communication Arrangement.

A Communication Arrangement describes which communication partners communicate with each other in the scenario, and how they communicate. Communication Arrangement is formed by combining the below artifacts:

1. Custom Communication Scenario

The setup of the communication (inbound and outbound) between SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP starts with the definition of a Custom Communication Scenario.
The Custom Communication Scenario contains the information for both inbound and outbound HTTP-calls.

  • Open the app Custom Communication Scenarios in the Launchpad and create a new Custom Communication Scenario with the name YY1_BPWORKFLOW.

  • On the tab Inbound Services, you create an entry for your Custom Business Object: Press Add button to add inbound service “YY1_BONUSPLAN_CDS” and click select button. 

     

  • On the Outbound Services tab you create an entry for the workflow capability on SAP BTP with the URL-Path /workflow-service/rest/v1

    Switch to Outbound Service section Press Add to start outbound service creation. A pop-up opens. Enter following data into the input fields.

Field Label

Field Value

Description

Outbound Service for SAP Build Process Automation

Outbound Service ID

BONUSAPPROVAL (prefix YY1_ and suffix _REST are added automatically)

URL Path

/workflow-service/rest/v1(service specific part of before gotten service’s end point)

 

Press Create to finish the outbound service creation.

Press Save and Publish to finish the custom communication scenario creation.

2. Communication User:

If you want to access the BonusPlan OData service (Inbound) you also need to setup an authentication method. You can use several methods like e.g.­., Basic Authentication, Certificate or OAuth.

In this case we are choosing Basic Authentication for which you need to setup a Communication User. Open the app Maintain Communication Users from the Communication Management group and create there a new user with username, description, and password.

3. Create Communication Arrangement

You will now create a communication arrangement between the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and SAP Build process automation using service key of SAP Build process automation Instance.

For this, follow the following steps:

a) Get SAP Build Process Automation Service Key

  • In the SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your space. See Navigate to Orgs and Spaces.
  • From the navigation pane, choose Instances and Subscriptions.
  • Click the name of your SAP Build Process Automation instance.
  • From the navigation pane, choose Service Keys.
  • Select one entry and copy the service key.

b) Create Communication Arrangement using Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0863

Communication Scenario: Communication Scenario is a design time description of how two communication partners communicate with each other. It consists of technical information like inbound and/or outbound services example OData or SOAP as well as supported authentication methods. There are various standard communication scenarios provided by SAP which are ready to use, like in this blog we would be using SAP_COM_0863 (SAP Process Automation Integration).

  • Open the Communication Arrangement app in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system’s Fiori Launchpad
  • Click on New to create communication arrangement.
  • Choose Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0863. SAP_COM_0863 is a ready-to-use communication scenario provided by SAP for integration with SAP Build Process Automation.
  • Select a Communication User (of which you know the password) which has created in the previous step or create a new one to use for inbound communication. 

  • Click on Additional Properties, set given value, and click Close.

       Consumer Type (Workflow) – DEFAULT

The consumer type is used to identify the default communication arrangement i.e., if the SAP Build Process Automation Communication Arrangement has Consumer Type (Workflow) set to DEFAULT, then the system will try to trigger all workflows in SAP Build Process Automation by default. Therefore, default assignment can be done only on one Communication arrangement for a Consumer Type.

  • In Service key text area paste the SAP Build Process Automation instance service key that we retrieved as part of ‘Get SAP Build Process Automation Service Key’ (See Section 3.a) and choose Create.

c) Create Communication Arrangement for Custom Communication Scenario

As the last part the Custom Communication Arrangement brings all these previously created information together. It contains the information for both inbound and outbound HTTP calls.

Open the app Communication Arrangements and create there a new one based on the previously created Custom Communication Scenario YY1_BPWORKFLOW.

Here you save the inbound service URL for the destination configuration in BTP.

Build your Business Process Workflow with SAP Build Process Automation

Enable users to create forms, manage decision logic, and build, adapt, and organize process flows with the simplicity of drag-and-drop functionality. You will learn how to use forms to build Bonus Approval workflow in this blog. The Approver must review these Bonus Approval requests before approving or rejecting them. The requester will be notified of the decision.

1. Create destination to SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.    

In the SAP BTP cockpit, you need to create destinations to enable the communication to the S/4HANA Cloud system. In addition, the destination must be enabled for use in SAP Build Process Automation.

Procedure

In the SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your subaccount. See Navigate to Orgs and Spaces.

Choose Connectivity > Destination > Create Destination.

Choose Blank Template and enter the following data and click on Save.

Field

Value

Name

Enter the destination name.

Type

Select HTTP.

Description

Enter a description (SBPA to ABAP Callback Destination)

URL

Enter the API-URL saved during Communication Arrangement creation. If not saved access the communication arrangement

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

Select BasicAuthentication.

User

(Inbound) communication user used in the communication arrangement

Password

Set the password to the secret. Password of the (inbound) communication user used in the communication arrangement.

Additional Properties

Add the following properties:

“sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled” → true

“sap.build.usage” -> RAP

“sap.processautomation.enabled ” → true

  • Save your entries.

2. Add Callback destination in SAP Build process Automation.

  • Open the SAP Build Process automation from your subaccount.

Subaccount > Instances and Subscriptions > Subscriptions > SAP Build Process Automation

  • Click on SAP Build Process Automation to open the Application.
  • Navigate Control Tower > Destinations.
  • Select the destination that you have created in the SAP BTP cockpit and click Add.

3. Create a Business Process Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.

  • Launch SAP Build Process Automation application from the BTP and In the Lobby, choose Create.
  • Pick Build an Automated Process.
  • Select Business Process.

Business Process Projects are a collection of skills in SAP Build Process Automation. Projects are part of the internal business processes of a company and are defined based on business scenarios. A project can contain a set of processes, forms, automations and decisions, which are reusable artifacts.

  • In the Create a Business Process Project dialog box, do the following:
  • Enter a Project Name: custombonusplan
  • Enter a Short Description: custom bonus plan
  • Choose Create
  • Create a business process
  • A new tab opens with the newly created project.
  • In the Create Process dialog box, provide the following:
  • Enter a Name: bonus_change_wf
  • Enter a Description for your process: Process to handle bonus Workflow
  • Choose Create

4.  Create the data type for the API Trigger.

  • Go to your project and click on Create > Data Type.
  • Name the data type Bonus Context and click on Create.
  • Click on New Field to add new fields to the data type Bonus Context. Your final data type looks as below. Click Save to save the data type.

 

5. Create an API Trigger for the process.

Create an API trigger for the business process to be called from external system

Process triggers can be a form, such as a request form, an API call, where an external system starts the process or an Event. You may also start the instance with an event trigger which on-boards and listens to back-end events emitted from an external source system and reacts to such events by triggering artifacts such as processes and automations.

  • Open process bonus_change_wf.
  • To add an API trigger for the process, click on Add API trigger.
  • Choose Call an API as we are creating an API based trigger so that we can trigger the process from external system like S4HANA or SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

  • Enter the below details for the trigger. Choose Create.
  • Name: Bonus Approval Trigger
  • Description: Bonus Approval Trigger
  • Please note that the identifier will be filled by itself

6. Create input for the process.

  • Open the Process Details by clicking on the Canvas.
  • Choose Variables. Then choose Configure to configure process inputs.
  • In the Configure Process Inputs window, choose Add Input to add parameters.

        Add the following parameter and click on Apply.

Name

Type

Bonus Context

bonus context

Process Input Fields are now visible in the Process Details.

7. Create and Configure Approval Form.

Tasks are a part of any business process. SAP Build Process Automation helps you to create forms that are made available to the business users in their inboxes to take relevant action.

You can create these different forms using the Form Builder embedded in the process builder using different form field options. You can design and modify them without any coding.

  • Go to process bonus_change_wf and choose + below the Trigger.
  • Choose Approval from the available options.

  • Choose Blank Approval.
  • In the Create Approval dialog box, do the following:
  • In the Name field enter: Bonus Approval Form
  • In the Description field enter: Form to approve or reject Bonus.
  • Choose Create

  • Choose Open Editor of the Approval Form.
  • Design the Approval Form in the form builder by dragging-and-dropping fields into the form editor and configuring respective field settings.

Form Fields

Field Settings with Label

Headline 1

Bonus Request – Approval

Headline 2

Please approve or reject following request:

  • Add Input Fields, enter the labels and select the Read Only checkbox except comments text.

These fields will take value from the input of the process i.e., fields sent from the external system.

Form Fields

Field Settings with Label

Text

Employee Name

Text

Employee ID

Text

Bonus

Text

Release Status

Text

Target Revenue

Text

Comments

  • Save the Form.

Once the approval form is created its time to include it in the process flow and configure it to take the read only field values from process Input.

  • Go to process bonus_change_wf and choose + below the Trigger.
  • Choose Approval from the available options and select the approval form you created.

  • Select the Approval Form to configure the General information section.
  • Set the title of the task that will be displayed in the Inbox, enter the below details.

Fields

Values

Subject

Approve Bonus for employee employeename[Select employee name from Process Input]

Description

Bonus Approval

Priority

Medium

 

 

  • Set the Recipient, detail of the user who would receive the approval form in their inbox for approval.

Multiple valid email addresses can be added separated by commas in the below fields.

It is mandatory to fill one of the fields “users” or “groups”. We are filling “users” field here.

  • Similarly, go to the Inputs section and map the different input fields, which were marked as read-only in the approval form, by selecting the respective Process Content entry and save the process.

8. Create and Configure Action in the process flow.

Action is a feature in SAP Build Process Automation to connect processes with external systems, be it SAP or non-SAP systems.

In this blog, you will create an action project based on inbound service API (Here, we have created the inbound service URL for the custom business object in "Create Communication Arrangement for Custom Communication Scenario" section). You will use the PATCH call from SAP Build Process Automation action to the instance of the custom business object to notify if the bonus request has been Approved or Rejected on completion of workflow.

  • Create an Action Project.
  • Open SAP Build Lobby with your username and password, then do the following:

Under Connectors, select Actions.

  • Select ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model option from the list and select “CUSTBONUSPLAN” API which we have created

                

  • In the Create an Action project popup, do the following:
  • Enter the Project Name as Workflow Notification.
  • Enter the Description as Workflow Completion Notification.
  • Click Create.

As soon as the Bonus Notification Action gets created, SAP Build Actions will automatically open and the Add Actions to Bonus Notification pop up will appear.

In the YY1_BONUSPLAN entity, you want to only update the Release status field. Hence, in the popup, you have to select the PATCH method of /YY1_BONUSPLAN({SAP_UUID}) API and then click Add button.

  • As S/4HANA APIs need CSRF token, you need to enable it. Select Settings icon.

In the Project Settings window select CSRF and do the following:

    • Enable CSRF by selecting YES.
    • Under the Token Fetch End Point, enter: /

After that you can also test your action by navigating to “Test” Tab and provide the input values in the “Test Input Values” section and click the Test button. It should give “204” response and the test data should be reflected in the instance of custom business object.

  • Save the Action Project and Release the action Project.

You will now release the action project to create version(s) and then publish a selected version in the action repository. It is then these published actions that can be used in different processes and applications to connect to external systems.

  • To release a version of the action project, click Release from top-right corner.
  • Enter the Release Notes: “Bonus Completion Notification V1”

  • Publish Action Project.

Once the action project is released, you can then publish any release version of the action by clicking Publish to Library from top-right corner.

9. Add Action Project to Business Process.

Add the action in the business process to connect to send the workflow completion notification back to the system that triggered the workflow. You can do this by choosing the relevant action from the action’s library, and then configuring the action’s parameters.

  • Go to lobby and open project custombonusplan. Navigate to open the process builder canvas for bonus_change_wf and click on expand ‘>’ for the Bonus Approval form.
  • To add the callback action once the form is Approved, click on the + on the Approve branch.

From the options select Action.

In the Browse Action library pop up, enter the action name that you just created like in this case ‘Bonus Notification’ in the Search field, to find the actions published by you in the library. Once you find your action project click on Add.

10. Configure Action for Approval

  • On clicking the add button your action will be added to the business flow and a pop-up window will open to configure the action settings for destination, Input, and output parameter mapping.
  • In General tab of action parameters, expand Destination variable and choose +Create Destination Variable.
  • In the pop-up window for Create Destination Variable, fill in the below values and choose  Create.

  • Click Inputs tab and map each input to the actual process content.

11. Release and Deploy Business Process Project.

To Run the Business Process Project, you need to first release and deploy it.

Till now you have created and saved your business process project along with the callback action. You will now release and deploy your project. The release process allows for semantic version control with format X.Y.Z, where you will be able to increment your project release version based on a major version change (X), minor version change (Y), or bug/patch version change (Z). Deploying your project will allow you to set the proper parameters like destination, if necessary, to allow for project execution.

Releasing a project creates a version or snapshot of the changes.

  • In the Process Builder, choose Release on the top right corner.

Add a Version Comment if needed and choose Release.

The project released successfully and is ready to be deployed.

12. Deploy the released Business Process Project.

You can deploy Business Process projects from each released version of the project in the Process Builder or through the Lobby. Deploying the project makes it available for others to use it. Please note that you can only deploy a released version of the project.

  • From the released version of the Business Process project in the Process Builder, choose Deploy > Select Environment Public > choose Deploy.

You will now set the runtime variables. From the drop-down select the destination CUSTBONUSPLAN and click Deploy.

13. Get the workflow definition ID and payload structure from SAP Build process Automation.

Once you have successfully deployed the business process with an API trigger, you can view the API trigger in the Control Tower section.

You can go to this view by choosing Control Tower and then the Environments tile. Choose your environment and then the Triggers view and Select public.

You can filter your triggers by: Project name. 

Select the view option after clicking the three dots button in the Actions column from the above diagram which should open a dialog with the definitionId and payload structure which can be used in “Implement Logic for a Custom Business Object” steps to trigger the process from custom business object’s Request Approval action.

End to End Test

After finalizing all configuration steps, you can test the scenario end to end.

To test the workflow request from the custom Business Object, Open the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system’s Fiori Launchpad with your developer user and Start typing Bonus Plans in the Launchpad search and open the App from the results.

To Create an instance of bonus plan click Create button.

Enter some test data.

Save the Bonus Plan by clicking the Create button.

Now you can see the created bonus plan instance details with the Release Status Not Released in the Detail page.

Trigger the approval workflow via the button Request Approval. This sends out the HTTP request to SAP BTP SAP Build Process Automation workflow trigger.

Open the SAP Build Process Automation application from the BTP and click My Inbox button from the Lobby.

You can see the result on the My Inbox app of SAP Build Process Automation, where the counter is increased by one.

 

Add a decision note and click on Approve.

Back on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud side you refresh the BonusPlan UI or start the app again.
There you see the changed Release Status which is now Released.

Monitoring and Troubleshooting:

SAP Build Process Automation provides different applications for monitoring and managing different process skills. The applications include Process and Workflow Instances, Automation Jobs, Acquired Events, and more. These applications are available under the Monitor tab.

  • Login to SAP Build Process Automation.
  • Navigate to Monitoring > Processes and Workflow Instances
  • Choose your project name from the drop down > Select the project name.

Copy the entire text in ID highlighted below, that is your Workflow Definition ID.

entry.title: Why S/4HANA Extensibility Options are crucial.
entry.content:
I have worked for almost 30 years in the SAP ERP space and have seen many innovations in SAP software. Although there have been many changes during those years, the challenges in my job remain almost the same. As a developer, enterprise architect, SAP mentor, speaker, teacher, and advisor, I ask myself: ‘How can I add additional value for my customers out of their ERP system?’. The answer is always the same: Focus on the customer's unique requirements because SAP will always be a best-practice solution.

With this answer in mind, I researched SAP's strategy for the public edition of S/4HANA Cloud. I found that SAP thought about the customer's unique requirements and not only to help their customers. SAP’s plan to become a cloud company without losing the current customer base, which runs the ERP on-premise, was the most significant driver. Moving to the cloud for the current customer base is not that easy due to the high investments for their customers due to many custom enhancements for their current running business systems. And SAP devised a solution to overcome these customers’ needs: S/4HANA Extensibility Options.

This prompts me to rephrase my question: ‘How can I get more value from S/4HANA with SAP Extensibility options? ‘I will try to answer this question in this article series.

In Part 1, I will explain why SAP Extensibility options are critical to SAP strategy and why customers benefit from this capability.

In Part 2, I will discuss these extension possibilities in more depth.

In part 3, I will explain which option is best for third-party cloud vendors and SAP add-on solution providers to integrate their solutions with S/4HANA for the long term. 

Introduction

From the early days, SAP customers built extensions on top of their SAP ERP systems, which added a lot of value but also caused IT departments a lot of headaches.
